transcript:
SOURCE: Fathom (direct) — https://fathom.video/calls/701182214

Growth, Profit, & Freedom Roadmap Discovery Call — June 8, 2026
Sales Rep: Jonathan Farace | Firm: Pilkington Immigration
Participants: Andrew Maloney (majority owner), Brian Gill (marketing/client relations)

FIRM OVERVIEW:
- Immigration law firm (Canadian + U.S.) founded 15 years ago by Andrew Maloney (majority owner; 3 owners total)
- ~30 employees, 8-9 attorneys
- Offices: Guelph ON (HQ), Toronto, Burlington, Kitchener, Orlando FL (recently acquired Silser Law ~$1.5M/yr)
- 55 websites under pilkingtonimmigration.com umbrella (local sites per location) + ~45 Google Business Profile listings across Canada and U.S.
- Revenue: ~$3M USD/year — flatlined for ~2 years after Google algorithm update hit in summer 2024
- Prior trajectory: grew from $2.2M → $2.4M → $3M, then stalled

LEAD GENERATION / MARKETING:
- Current spend: ~$30K/month total ($20K SEO, $10K Google PPC ad spend) — with existing agency for 15 years
- Google Ads ROI: was 1.5:1 a year ago, now below 0.5:1 (losing money); lots of spam/low-quality leads
- ~30 Google Business Profiles suspended summer 2024 due to Google algorithm change; all restored but ads performance never recovered
- SEO: organic leads actually performing well and growing; Brian actively cultivates 5-star Google reviews
- Social media: essentially zero — just created Facebook page, YouTube channel, and LinkedIn presence; never run paid social
- Content: one article/video per month; manual WordPress process across 55 sites is cumbersome
- Lead sources YTD by revenue (Canadian $): Organic #1 (nearly double next), Repeat Clients #2, Referrals #3; Ads a distant fraction
- Top case types YTD revenue: E-Visa $325K, Spousal Sponsorship $250K, Canadian Citizenship $200K, U.S. Waiver $160K, U.S. L1 Visa $150K, U.S. Family-Based Sponsorship $115K

INTAKE / CONVERSION:
- Inbound calls → AI reception service → routes to lawyer based on issue/location/language → attorney follows up within a few hours
- ~80-100 new clients/month (140-160 invoices/month, ~75% new clients)
- 20-25 paid Zoom consultations/month at $300 each (for non-obvious buyers)
- No dedicated intake/sales team — attorneys handle initial follow-up and conversion
- No data tracked on lead-to-consult-to-client conversion rates
- Average case values: Waivers $1,500-2,000; Citizenship ~$2,000; Family sponsorship $4,000-5,000; E-visas $7,000-8,000; EB5 ~$20,000

TEAM / OPERATIONS:
- Andrew still heavily in-the-business day-to-day; wants to move toward more of an owner/CEO role
- Has never worked with a business coach or outside advisor
- Open to coaching but it was not top of mind — primary ask was marketing growth
- Brian manages marketing and client relations; manually handles content uploads
- Some attorneys are strong closers; others (e.g., French-speaking lawyer) not suited to sales calls

GROWTH GOALS:
- Wants to grow but prefers proving ROI in a few markets first, then scaling ("see it work, then do it times five")
- Has capacity for ~30% more clients without new hires; would hire to scale beyond that
- Top opportunity markets: mid-size cities where currently on page 2 (London ON, Kitchener, Edmonton, Vaughan, Brampton, Markham, Ottawa, Calgary)
- Huge markets (Toronto, Vancouver) seen as expensive/competitive; some smaller markets already maxed out
- Waivers were previously $750K/yr in revenue; now down ~2/3 due to Trump-era policies and 1-year processing times
- Interested in supplementing (not necessarily replacing) current marketing agency

COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE:
- Medium-to-high competition in major Canadian metros
- Competitive advantage: bilingual service (French), dual Canada/U.S. immigration expertise, fast response time, repeat client base
- Unique multi-location presence with 55 microsites and 45 GMB listings

FATHOM SUMMARY: Growth has flatlined at ~$3M USD/year since a 2024 Google algorithm update crippled the firm's primary lead source, Google Ads. Google Ads are now unprofitable (ROI < 1.0). Firm has strong organic and referral channels but no social media strategy. Significant opportunity in mid-size Canadian markets where they are on page 2. Andrew open to growing but wants proof of ROI before scaling.
